evals.prompt: First run `python3 evals/files/setup_fixture.py queue-defect` to create the supplied staged implementation and external artifacts. Independently review task `QR-12` in `/work/queue-runner` using `docs/tasks/QR-12.md`, `/work/task-runs/QR-12/preflight.md`, and `/work/task-runs/QR-12/worker-result.json`. Treat the worker's passing unit/state claim as submitted evidence. Independently execute the required two consecutive public invocations against persisted state in an authorized disposable directory. Write only `/work/task-runs/QR-12/acceptance.md`; do not fix implementation or update tracker state.
evals.expected_output: A read-only acceptance report with a blocking located finding tied to the repeated-flow AC, independently observed first-state-change-plus-second-invocation evidence, and verdict `CHANGES_REQUESTED` routed to the same bounded implementation thread.
expectations: The report treats the passing worker tests and result as claims and independently exercises the real `runner run-next` public entry point twice through persisted state.
It records the first legitimate claim/state change and the second invocation's failure to claim `TASK-2`, rather than inferring repeated behavior from unit or state tests.
A P1 or P2 finding includes a current implementation location, triggering state, consequence, evidence, affected AC, and smallest corrective direction without a patch.
The report uses exactly verdict `CHANGES_REQUESTED` and routes to the same `bounded-task-implementer` thread followed by a new acceptance review.
Only the authorized acceptance report is written; source, tests, task artifacts, tracker state, and task-orchestrator are not modified.

evals.prompt: First run `python3 evals/files/setup_fixture.py exporter-accept` to create the supplied staged implementation, external artifacts, and real pre-existing `MM notes/local.md` user state. Run task acceptance review for `EXP-08` in `/work/exporter` using `/work/task-runs/EXP-08/preflight.md` and its worker result. Confirm the separate index/worktree fingerprints for the user path, reconstruct only the two allowed task changes, and independently exercise public resume plus retry in disposable state. Write `/work/task-runs/EXP-08/acceptance.md` and do not advance the task.
evals.expected_output: A concise clean acceptance report that corroborates every AC at module and real-entry levels, records deterministic retry/resume evidence and clean scope, states `No findings.`, and returns `ACCEPT` routed only to task-orchestrator.
expectations: The report reconstructs a clean scoped diff from the preflight baseline, separately confirms the unchanged index/worktree fingerprints of pre-existing `MM` user work, and confirms that only allowed task-owned paths changed.
Every AC has corroborated module/state evidence plus real public `exportctl resume` evidence, including negative no-duplicate behavior.
The lifecycle row and independent probe record the first export, legitimate checkpoint state, resumed or retried occurrence, and externally observed result.
The findings section says `No findings.` and does not manufacture a defect when the evidence is clean.
The report uses exactly verdict `ACCEPT`, states any residual limits, and routes only to `task-orchestrator` without updating tracker or orchestration state.

evals.prompt: First run `python3 evals/files/setup_fixture.py scheduler-inconclusive` to create the supplied staged implementation and external artifacts. Review task `SCH-09` in `/work/scheduler` from `docs/tasks/SCH-09.md`, `/work/task-runs/SCH-09/preflight.md`, and `/work/task-runs/SCH-09/worker-result.json`. The AC requires deterministic proof that two workers cannot claim the same job, but the preflight-recorded helper has been removed after preflight. The ordinary unit suite passes, while the supplied run policy forbids inventing a replacement or using a live scheduler. Write `/work/task-runs/SCH-09/acceptance.md` without repairing anything.
evals.expected_output: An `INCONCLUSIVE` acceptance report that identifies the stale/invalid helper evidence and missing decisive concurrency capability, refuses to substitute unit tests or create a helper, and routes to task-preflight or the prerequisite owner before review can be repeated.
expectations: The report identifies the preflight helper digest mismatch and current absence as exact stale/current-state evidence.
It marks the real concurrency AC unverified and does not treat the passing unit suite or worker result as sufficient proof.
It does not create or replace the helper, edit tests or source, run an unauthorized live probe, or ask the implementer to discover around the gap.
The report uses exactly verdict `INCONCLUSIVE`, not `ACCEPT` or `CHANGES_REQUESTED`.
The next route names `task-preflight` or the prerequisite capability owner with the smallest action, followed by a new acceptance review.

evals.prompt: First run `python3 evals/files/setup_fixture.py exporter-restaged-stale` to create the supplied staged implementation and external artifacts. Review `EXP-08` from `/work/task-runs/EXP-08/preflight.md` and its worker result. After preflight, another process replaced only the index blob for pre-existing `MM notes/local.md`; the exact short status and worktree bytes still match. Write only `/work/task-runs/EXP-08/acceptance.md` and do not repair or normalize Git state.
evals.expected_output: An `INCONCLUSIVE` report that detects the separately changed index blob, refuses to absorb or reset user work, and routes to fresh task-preflight despite matching worktree bytes and short-status text.
expectations: The review compares the preflight index-blob digest independently from the matching worktree digest and short-status text.
It classifies the changed pre-existing index state as unexplained and the baseline as unsafe to reconstruct.
It uses exactly verdict `INCONCLUSIVE` and routes to fresh `task-preflight` before any renewed acceptance review.
It does not reset, restage, overwrite, or claim ownership of `notes/local.md` and does not review a guessed scoped diff as authoritative.
Only the authorized external acceptance report is written.

evals.prompt: First run `python3 evals/files/setup_fixture.py ledger-defects` to create the staged implementation in `/work/ledger-runner`. Perform a guided acceptance review of `docs/tasks/LED-04.md` against the current diff. There is no preflight packet, worker JSON, run policy, or report path; report inline and remain read-only. Do not rely on the happy-path test alone: independently exercise selected versus non-selected task states, zero/one/two active owners, latest-attempt ordering, exact ready-set behavior, stopped-parent/running-child coherence, boolean revisions, and string `completed_task_ids`.
evals.expected_output: A concise guided `CHANGES_REQUESTED` review that proceeds without orchestrator artifacts and locates the ledger validator's interacting-state and type-strictness defects with independent evidence.
expectations: The review proceeds in guided mode without requiring a preflight packet, worker result, digest ledger, policy file, or durable report path.
Independent probes demonstrate that a valid selected task can hide an invalid non-selected running task and that ready/stopped states accept incoherent sibling states.
The review checks exact cardinality and shows that the running state can admit more than one running task.
The review catches non-latest active attempts, inexact ready sets, boolean revisions, and string `completed_task_ids` as applicable defects.
The inline human-readable verdict is `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, contains concrete located findings and AC evidence, and requires a fresh review after correction without editing the repository.
